defs: One who, or that which, gives a name; origin or source of a name.
quotes: This opinion that Aram . . . was the father and denomination of the Syrians in general.

defs: That number placed below the line in common fractions which shows into how many parts the integer or unit is divided.
notes: ☞ Thus, in , 5 is the denominator, showing that the integer is divided into five parts; and the numerator, 3, shows how many parts are taken.

defs: That part of any expression under a fractional form which is situated below the horizontal line signifying division.
notes: ☞ In this sense, the denominator is not necessarily a number, but may be any expression, either positive or negative, real or imaginary.
